drills.name: Ground Ball Fundamentals
drills.description: Coach rolls firm grounders, alternating forehand and backhand. Field, footwork, throw to the bucket target.
drills.coachingPoints: Fielding triangle: feet wide, glove out front. Right, left, throw. Work through the ball, never around it.

drills.name: Tee Work: Bat Path
drills.description: Hitters rotate through tee stations into a net: middle tee, inside tee moved up, outside tee moved back. Five swings per station.
drills.coachingPoints: Short to the ball, long through it. Match the tee depth to the pitch location.

drills.name: Front Toss Rhythm Rounds
drills.description: Coach front-tosses from behind an L-screen at game-like rhythm, mixing speeds slightly. Hitters take rounds of six, calling ball or strike before swinging.
drills.coachingPoints: See it early, load on time. Let your eyes decide before your hands do.

drills.name: Opposite-Field Rounds
drills.description: Front toss on the outer half only. Hitters get a round of six and every ball must go to the opposite-field side of second base or the round doesn't count.
drills.coachingPoints: Let the outside pitch travel deep and drive it where it's pitched.

drills.name: Two-Strike Battle
drills.description: Every at-bat starts 1-2. Coach mixes locations and takes; hitter earns a point for any ball put in play, loses one for a called strike three or chase.
drills.coachingPoints: Choke up, widen out, shorten the swing. Win the pitch, not the highlight.

drills.name: First-Step Reads off the Bat
drills.description: Fielders at their positions; coach hits live fungos in any direction. Players read contact and take only their first three steps at full speed, then reset.
drills.coachingPoints: Read the angle of the bat, not the flight of the ball. Your first step decides the play.

drills.name: Pop-Up Priority
drills.description: Coach lofts pop-ups into the seams between infielders and outfielders. Players call it by priority rules, everyone else peels off loudly.
drills.coachingPoints: Call it three times, early and loud. The outfielder coming in owns the seam.

drills.name: Four-Corner Quick Throws
drills.description: Four lines form a square 60 feet apart. Ball moves around the horn with quick transfers; on the whistle, reverse direction. Race two squares against each other.
drills.coachingPoints: Catch moving toward your target. Glove to pocket in one motion.

drills.name: Long Toss Ladder
drills.description: Pairs start at 60 feet and back up ten feet after every five clean throws, out to each player's max distance, then walk it back in with firm low throws.
drills.coachingPoints: Stay tall and finish out front. Stretch the distance, never the mechanics.

drills.name: Bullpen Command Grid
drills.description: Catcher splits the zone into four quadrants; pitcher throws a 20-pitch pen calling the quadrant before each pitch. Score a point per hit target.
drills.coachingPoints: Commit to the quadrant before you lift your leg. Aim small, miss small.

drills.name: Dry Mechanics Mirror
drills.description: Pitchers pair up without a ball and work delivery checkpoints: balance point, hand break, stride, finish. The partner freezes them at each one to check positions.
drills.coachingPoints: Pause at the balance point in full control. If you can't hold it, you rushed it.

drills.name: Pickoffs vs Live Leads
drills.description: Pitcher works from the stretch with a live runner at first taking real leads. Pitcher mixes pitches home, holds, and pickoff throws; runner works reads and dives back.
drills.coachingPoints: Vary your looks and your timing. The pattern is what runners steal, not the pitch.

drills.name: Steal Jumps off the Pitcher
drills.description: Runners at first read a pitcher's live moves: break on first movement home, dive back on a pickoff. Three reads each, rotate runners through.
drills.coachingPoints: Read the heel and the front shoulder. Cross over and go, no false step.

drills.name: First-to-Third Reads
drills.description: Runner at first, coach fungos base hits to different outfield spots. Runner reads the ball's angle rounding second and decides to take third or hold, with a base coach signaling.
drills.coachingPoints: Pick up the ball at the cut of second and find your coach. Decide early, run hard through the decision.

drills.name: Sliding Technique Station
drills.description: On grass or a sliding mat, players run at three-quarter speed and work bent-leg slides into a loose base, progressing to slides to either side of the bag.
drills.coachingPoints: Sit, don't jump. Hands up, slide past the tag side of the base.

drills.name: Home-to-First Sprints
drills.description: Full-speed swings off an imaginary pitch, then sprint through first base on a timer. Two rounds: straight through on a single, banana turn on a double.
drills.coachingPoints: Run through the base at full speed and hit the front half of the bag.

drills.name: Base Path Agility Relays
drills.description: Two teams race a cone circuit shaped like the base paths: sprint, round the cone tight, shuffle a segment, backpedal home, tag the next runner.
drills.coachingPoints: Lean into every turn and cut the corner tight. Wide turns lose the race.

drills.name: Cutoffs and Relays
drills.description: Coach fungos balls to the outfield gaps with a called play; outfielder hits the cutoff man, who relays to the called base. Rotate outfield, cutoff, and base positions.
drills.coachingPoints: Line up the relay with your hands up and call the base. Two low, hard throws beat one rainbow.

drills.name: Freeze Situations
drills.description: Full defense on the field. Coach calls outs and runners, fungos a live ball, and yells freeze after the play. Every player explains where they went and why.
drills.coachingPoints: Know your job before the pitch. The play is decided before the ball is hit.

drills.name: Bunt Defense Reps
drills.description: Infield aligns in bunt coverage with runners on; coach rolls or bunts live balls and the defense executes the called play to first, second, or third.
drills.coachingPoints: Charge under control, field with two hands, and listen for the base call before you throw.
